recalls.Description: 509 has issued a recall for several models of Tactical helmets, including the Tactical 3.0 (Youth, Mountain, and Trail) and Tactical 2.0 helmets, due to insufficient crash protection that may lead to head injuries. The affected helmets were manufactured in 2023 and include various sizes and colors. Additionally, helmets with Fidlock® systems, such as the Tactical 2.0, Altitude 2.0, Delta R3L, Delta R4, Delta V, Mach III, Mach IV, and Mach V, produced from 2020 onward, are also part of this recall. Each helmet features the 509 logo or the Polaris branding, indicating their respective models.
recalls.Title: 509 Recalls Tactical Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach 509 at 877-743-3509 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email support@ride509.com. More details are available at https://ride509.com/pages/recalls. Polaris Industries can be contacted at 800-765-2747 during the same hours, or online at www.polaris.com under "Product Safety News". Polaris' owner connections team can be reached at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Mountain Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Trail Helmet
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 3.0 Youth Helmet
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 3.0 Mountain Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 2.0 Small/Medium Helmets
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 2.0 Small/Medium Helmets
Recalled 509 and 509 for Polaris Helmets with Fidlock®
Correct Fidlock
Incorrect Fidlock Strap Fastener

Injuries.Name: The firm received one incident where the rider's helmet became detached during a crash, resulting in a concussion.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.ride509.com and www.polaris.com, as well as through 509 and Polaris dealers nationwide from January 2020 through May 2025, priced between $140 and $900.

Importers.Name: Teton Outfitters LLC, dba 509, a Polaris subsidiary, of Rigby, Idaho

Hazards.Name: Insufficient protection in the event of a crash, increasing the risk of head injury.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled helmets immediately. Contact 509 for refund options or replacements for Tactical 3.0 and 2.0 helmets. Polaris customers can choose refunds or replacements for their helmets. No proof of purchase is required. Discard all recalled helmets after confirming their status and following destruction instructions provided online.

recalls.metaDescription: 509 recalls Tactical helmets due to head injury risks. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: Polaris has announced a recall affecting all VINs of the 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic and the 2025 Pro XD Kinetic recreational off-highway vehicles (ROVs). The Ranger XP Kinetic models are available in camouflage and white, while the Pro XD Kinetic models feature gray plastic body panels with a black frame. Each vehicle is designed with three seating configurations and is marked with "POLARIS" on the front grille. The VIN can be found on the left rear frame, beneath the cargo box. This recall also includes accessory charger service parts with specific part numbers.
recalls.Title: Polaris Issues Recall for Ranger XP Kinetic and Pro XD Kinetic Off-Highway Vehicles Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at www.polaris.com under "Product Safety News" or by visiting https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to verify if their VIN is affected. Polaris' owner connections team can also be contacted at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through an online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic ROVs
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,900 ROVs and 185 accessory charger service parts/kits (In addition, about 330 ROVs were sold in Canada)

Recalled Polaris Model Year 2025 Pro XD Kinetic
Recalled Polaris RANGER and Pro XD VIN Location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 47 reports of filter failures with the recalled ROVs, including six reports of melting/fire and four reports of sparking/smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Available at Polaris dealers nationwide from April 2023 to March 2025, priced between $24,000 and $37,500.

Hazards.Name: Potential fire hazard due to water leakage through a cracked filter

Remedies.Name: Free repair available through authorized Polaris dealers

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls Ranger XP Kinetic and Pro XD Kinetic ROVs due to fire hazards. Check your VIN and learn about free repairs.

recalls.Description: Polaris Industries is recalling specific VINs of the 2023-2025 Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar and Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar Crew recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles, available in various colors including black, blue, and camouflage, feature three and six-seat configurations with 'POLARIS' marked on the front grille. The VIN can be found on the left rear frame beneath the cargo box on the driver's side.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar Models Due to Seat Belt Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Visit www.polaris.com and select 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' column, or check recalls at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/recalls. Consumers can also reach the owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through the online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Injuries.Name: The firm has identified two vehicles with loose seat belt fasteners. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Available at Polaris dealers across the country from August 2022 through March 2025, priced between $28,000 and $38,000.

Hazards.Name: Improperly tightened seat belt anchor fasteners can lead to seat belt detachment, increasing the risk of injury in a crash.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ROVs immediately and contact an authorized Polaris dealer for a free inspection and repair. Consumers may operate the vehicles prior to inspection if they ensure the seat belt fasteners are secure, following the Seat Belt Fastener Verification Instructions on the Polaris website. Polaris has reached out to registered owners directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar ROVs due to a seat belt hazard. Contact Polaris for inspection and repair details.

recalls.Description: Polaris Industries has issued a recall for specific VINs of the 2022-2025 RZR Pro R and RZR Pro R 4 recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles are available in various colors including black, blue, gray, green, and orange, and come in both two-seat and four-seat configurations. The front grille features the 'POLARIS' logo, while 'POLARIS' and 'RZR' are printed on the sides, with the model name also displayed on the chassis. The VIN can be found on the vehicle's frame in the left rear wheel well.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls RZR Pro R and RZR Pro R 4 Off-Road Vehicles Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at www.polaris.com by clicking on 'Product Safety News' in the 'Safety' section or by visiting https://www.polaris.com/en-us/recalls/off-road/polaris-recalls-some-model-year-2022-2025-rzr-pro-r-and-rzr-pro-4-vehicles/. Consumers can also email Owner.Connections@polaris.com or access an online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Injuries.Name: The firm has identified two vehicles with missing fasteners and one vehicle with a fastener that was not tightened. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Polaris dealers across the country from November 2021 to January 2025, priced between $32,000 and $50,000.

Importers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Potential injury risk due to loose or missing fasteners on the vehicle's rollover protective structure (ROPS) pillar joints.

Remedies.Name: Owners should cease use of the recalled ROVs and contact an authorized Polaris dealer for a complimentary inspection and repair. While waiting for the repair, consumers may operate the ROVs if they verify the security of the ROPS fasteners by following the instructions on the Polaris website. Polaris will directly notify registered owners.

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls RZR Pro R and RZR Pro R 4 vehicles due to safety hazards. Contact Polaris for inspections and repairs.

recalls.Description: Honda is recalling accessory heaters designed for the 2023-2025 Pioneer 700, 2022-2024 Pioneer 1000, and 2022-2024 Talon 1000 models. These heaters, sold separately from the vehicles, feature a control panel mounted on the dashboard that allows users to adjust air speed, airflow direction, and temperature. Owners can verify if their vehicle is affected by entering the VIN at https://powersports.honda.com/recalls. The VIN can be found in the owner's manual under the Technical Information - Vehicle Identification section.
recalls.Title: Honda Recalls Accessory Heaters for 2022-2025 Pioneer and Talon Side-by-Sides Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact American Honda at 866-784-1870 from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://powersports.honda.com and select "Recall Information" for further details.

Products.Name: Accessory Heaters for 2022-2025 Honda Pioneer 700, Pioneer 1000, and Talon 1000

Images.Caption: Recalled accessory heater system and control panel
Compatible Honda Pioneer and Honda Talon vehicles

Injuries.Name: Honda has received three reports of the recalled accessory heaters overheating, including two incidents resulting in fires and vehicle damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Sold through authorized Honda Powersports dealers across the country from January 2022 to September 2024, priced between $900 and $1,040.

Distributors.Name: American Honda Motor Co. Inc, of Torrance, California

Hazards.Name: Fire and burn hazards due to overheating

Remedies.Name: Stop using the heaters and contact an authorized Honda Powersports dealer for a free repair; Honda will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Honda recalls accessory heaters for Pioneer and Talon models due to fire hazards. Check your vehicle's compatibility and seek repairs.

recalls.Description: Polaris Industries has issued a recall for specific VINs of the 2024-2025 Ranger XD 1500 and Ranger Crew XD 1500 recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles, available in various colors including camouflage, red, silver, and titanium, come in three- and six-seat configurations and feature the "POLARIS" logo on the front grille. The VIN can be found on the left rear frame under the cargo box. Additionally, accessory doors with part numbers 2212292, 2212291, 2638498, 2638499, 2638500, 2638501, 2889075, or 2889076 purchased prior to November 11, 2024, are also included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls 2024-2025 Ranger XD 1500 and Crew XD 1500 ROVs Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Visit www.polaris.com and click on "Product Safety News" under the "Safety" section or go to https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to check if your vehicle's VIN is affected. You can also reach Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

recalls.Description:
Polaris Industries has issued a recall for specific VINs of the Model Year 2024 RANGER XD 1500 and RANGER CREW XD 1500 recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles are available in camouflage, red, and silver colors and come in three and six-seat configurations, featuring the 'POLARIS' logo on the front grille. The VIN can be found on the left rear frame under the cargo box. Additionally, the recall includes 4,500 lb. and 6,000 lb. winches sold as accessories, identified by part numbers 2889275 and 2889276.

The winch wiring in these ROVs may overheat during use, which presents a fire hazard and poses a serious injury risk to riders and passengers. To ensure safety, consumers are advised to stop using the winch immediately and contact an authorized Polaris dealer for a complimentary repair to verify the winch ground connection.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls Ranger XD 1500 ROVs and Winches Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.polaris.com and click on 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' section. You can also check for recalls using your vehicle identification number (VIN) at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/. For further inquiries, email Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or access their online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2024 RANGER XD 1500 Recreational Off-Road Vehicle

Hazards.Name: Overheating winch wiring posing a fire hazard

The brand name CFMOTO is displayed on the front, while ZFORCE is printed on the side of the chassis. Hazards.Name: Shock absorber rod assembly detachment leading to suspension failure, increasing the risk of crash and tip-over.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled ROVs immediately and contact an authorized CFMOTO dealer to arrange a free repair. CFMOTO has issued a Stop Ride notice and is reaching out to all registered owners and dealers with further repair details.

recalls.metaDescription: CFMOTO recalls ZFORCE 950 ROVs due to crash risks. Stop using immediately and contact an authorized dealer for repairs.

recalls.Description:
Polaris Industries has issued a recall for its 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs) due to a crash hazard. These vehicles, available in camouflage and white, feature a three-seat configuration and have 'POLARIS' marked on the front grille. The model number and VIN can be found on the left rear frame beneath the cargo box on the driver's side. 
Consumers are urged to stop using the affected vehicles immediately. If the brake and throttle pedals are pressed at the same time, the throttle may deactivate. Releasing the brake could lead to unexpected vehicle movement, increasing the risk of a crash and serious injury. Polaris is actively notifying registered owners about this issue and is providing instructions for a free repair to update the vehicle control system.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic Off-Road Vehicles Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Polaris at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also visit www.polaris.com and click on 'Product Safety News' at the bottom of the page, or go to www.polaris.com/en-us/recalls to check if your vehicle identification number (VIN) is affected. Additionally, reach out to Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online forum at www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received eight reports of loss of throttle response. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Authorized Polaris dealers nationwide from April 2023 to September 2023, priced between $25,000 and $37,500

Hazards.Name: Risk of crash due to throttle deactivation when brake and throttle are pressed simultaneously

Remedies.Name: Free repair and update to vehicle control system

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic vehicles due to crash hazards. Immediate action required for safety.
